title: Marketing-sourced deals close 25% faster this week
anchor: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 51 days over the prior 11 weeks.
connect: Mid-market accounts drive 7 of 9 current-week wins.
body: Current week wins average 38 days to close versus 51 days over the prior 11 weeks. Mid-market accounts drive 7 of 9 current-week wins.
trace: Significant velocity improvement in marketing-sourced deals this week, concentrated in mid-market segment.

title: Enterprise win rate jumps 8 points in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals convert at 31% versus 22% over the trailing four quarters.
connect: Average deal size increased to $187K from $142K.
body: Q2 enterprise deals convert at 31% versus 22% over the trailing four quarters. Average deal size increased to $187K from $142K.
trace: Material improvement in enterprise conversion with higher ASP, suggesting positioning or qualification improvements.

title: Event-sourced deals accelerate by 24 days
anchor: Q1 event wins close in 45 days versus 70 days in Q4.
connect: SaaS Connect drives 14 of 23 Q1 event wins.
body: Q1 event wins close in 45 days versus 70 days in Q4. SaaS Connect drives 14 of 23 Q1 event wins.
trace: Event-sourced pipeline showing strong velocity improvement, with SaaS Connect as primary driver.

title: Mid-market SQLs up 50% in April
anchor: April produces 147 mid-market SQLs versus 98 monthly average in Q1.
connect: Q1 months ran consistently at 95-101 SQLs.
body: April produces 147 mid-market SQLs versus 98 monthly average in Q1. Q1 months ran consistently at 95-101 SQLs.
trace: Significant mid-market SQL volume increase in April, breaking from steady Q1 pattern.

title: ABM acceptance rate runs 20 points higher
anchor: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM.
connect: April volume: 70 ABM MQLs, 77 non-ABM MQLs.
body: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM. April volume: 70 ABM MQLs, 77 non-ABM MQLs.
trace: Clear qualification advantage for ABM channel with nearly equal volume distribution.

title: Paid social flips lead over paid search
anchor: Q2 paid social creates $620K pipeline versus $380K from paid search.
connect: Q1 ran opposite: paid search $720K, paid social $410K.
body: Q2 paid social creates $620K pipeline versus $380K from paid search. Q1 ran opposite: paid search $720K, paid social $410K.
trace: Channel mix reversal between Q1 and Q2, with paid social becoming the lead paid channel.

title: Digital ads run $84K under plan
anchor: Q2 digital spend tracks at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23.
connect: Two event budget reallocations total the $84K gap.
body: Q2 digital spend tracks at $256K versus $340K planned through April 23. Two event budget reallocations total the $84K gap.
trace: Under-spend in digital ads matches exactly with event budget reallocations to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit.

title: Branded search doubles over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search volume climbs from 1000 to 1921 with gains every week.
connect: Cumulative growth reaches 92% through April 19.
body: Weekly branded search volume climbs from 1000 to 1921 with gains every week. Cumulative growth reaches 92% through April 19.
trace: Consistent week-over-week branded search increases suggesting strong brand awareness momentum.

title: Atlas mentions lead Beacon by 630
anchor: April brand mentions: Atlas SaaS 1840, top competitor Beacon Systems 1210.
connect: Atlas holds 60% share of voice between the two brands.
body: April brand mentions: Atlas SaaS 1840, top competitor Beacon Systems 1210. Atlas holds 60% share of voice between the two brands.
trace: Clear brand mention leadership over primary competitor Beacon Systems in April.

title: Analyst attention increases 50% in two weeks
anchor: Last 14 days produce 9 analyst mentions versus 6 in the prior 30 days.
connect: Forrester and G2 each contribute 3 mentions in the recent window.
body: Last 14 days produce 9 analyst mentions versus 6 in the prior 30 days. Forrester and G2 each contribute 3 mentions in the recent window.
trace: Accelerating analyst coverage with Forrester and G2 as primary drivers.

title: Direct traffic crosses organic for the first time
anchor: April traffic share: direct 42.4%, organic search 36.4%.
connect: Direct first exceeded organic on April 15.
body: April traffic share: direct 42.4%, organic search 36.4%. Direct first exceeded organic on April 15.
trace: Milestone shift where direct traffic became the leading channel, crossing organic search mid-April.

title: Atlas Insights reference opt-ins reach 76%
anchor: Customer reference rates: Atlas Insights 75.6%, Atlas Workflow 71.1%.
connect: Atlas Connect lags at 27.5% opt-in rate.
body: Customer reference rates: Atlas Insights 75.6%, Atlas Workflow 71.1%. Atlas Connect lags at 27.5% opt-in rate.
trace: Strong reference willingness for core products but Connect shows low advocacy levels.

title: Named accounts show high-intent clustering
anchor: Three target accounts register 5+ high-intent events this week.
connect: Two of the three sit on the Named Accounts list of 50.
body: Three target accounts register 5+ high-intent events this week. Two of the three sit on the Named Accounts list of 50.
trace: High-intent activity concentrated in named target accounts, suggesting ABM targeting accuracy.

title: Atlas Assist launch locks calendar this week
anchor: Product launch scheduled for May 8 with campaign status ready.
connect: Calendar locks Friday April 24.
body: Product launch scheduled for May 8 with campaign status ready. Calendar locks Friday April 24.
trace: Atlas Assist launch on track with marketing campaign ready and calendar locking this week.

title: SDR capacity exceeds inbound flow by 68 leads
anchor: Current week SDR capacity: 210 versus inbound volume of 142.
connect: Trailing month averaged capacity 214, inbound 193.
body: Current week SDR capacity: 210 versus inbound volume of 142. Trailing month averaged capacity 214, inbound 193.
trace: SDR capacity currently above inbound volume, shift from typical near-capacity utilization.

title: Fintech dominates mid-market wins
anchor: Last 60 days: 8 of 12 mid-market wins come from fintech vertical.
connect: All 12 wins run Snowflake plus dbt stack.
body: Last 60 days: 8 of 12 mid-market wins come from fintech vertical. All 12 wins run Snowflake plus dbt stack.
trace: Strong fintech vertical concentration in mid-market wins with consistent tech stack profile.

title: Event spend shifts to SaaS Connect concentration
anchor: Budget reallocation: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit absorb $84K from digital ads.
connect: SaaS Connect already delivered 14 Q1 wins from events.
body: Budget reallocation: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit absorb $84K from digital ads. SaaS Connect already delivered 14 Q1 wins from events.
trace: Budget moving toward event concentration with SaaS Connect proving strong win generation.

title: Q2 pipeline mix inverts from Q1
anchor: Paid social leads paid search pipeline creation by $240K in Q2.
connect: Q1 gap ran $310K in paid search favor.
body: Paid social leads paid search pipeline creation by $240K in Q2. Q1 gap ran $310K in paid search favor.
trace: Complete channel performance reversal between quarters, with paid social taking lead pipeline role.

title: Brand momentum shows across three signals
anchor: Branded search up 92%, direct traffic leads organic, mentions up 52%.
connect: Direct traffic crossover happened April 15.
body: Branded search up 92%, direct traffic leads organic, mentions up 52%. Direct traffic crossover happened April 15.
trace: Multiple brand signals showing coordinated strength: search, traffic, and mention volume all positive.

title: Mid-market velocity advantage widens
anchor: Current week marketing-sourced wins: 7 mid-market of 9 total.
connect: Mid-market deals average 38-day close versus enterprise 31% win rate.
body: Current week marketing-sourced wins: 7 mid-market of 9 total. Mid-market deals average 38-day close versus enterprise 31% win rate.
trace: Mid-market showing both volume and velocity advantages in marketing-sourced pipeline.
